plugin_desc: MacroSAN storage-specific SNMP collection for MacroSAN SAN/storage arrays (MACROSAN-MIB, IANA PEN 35904). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects MacroSAN private OIDs for device health — controller CPU utilization (cpuRate, a 0-100 percentage), controller memory utilization (vmRate, a 0-100 percentage), per-fan status (fanState), per-power-supply status (powerState) and per-disk health status (diskHealthState). The fan, power-supply and disk-health raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault and the worst component is taken per device with max(). Supported brands/models — MacroSAN MS series SAN/unified storage arrays with SNMP enabled.
name: Storage

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the controller CPU utilization of the MacroSAN storage array (MACROSAN-MIB cpuRate), expressed as a percentage from 0 to 100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate controller overload, heavy IO processing, or background array rebuild and may slow down host IO response.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the controller memory utilization of the MacroSAN storage array (MACROSAN-MIB vmRate), expressed as a percentage from 0 to 100. Sustained high memory usage can indicate cache pressure or a memory leak and may degrade IO performance or trigger controller failover.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan health of the MacroSAN storage array, derived from the per-fan status table (MACROSAN-MIB fanState, one row per fan). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other state is treated as a fault, and the worst fan is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a failed fan that risks overheating the controller enclosure.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the MacroSAN storage array, derived from the per-power-supply status table (MACROSAN-MIB powerState, one row per power supply). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other state is treated as a fault, and the worst supply is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a power-supply fault or loss of power redundancy.

metrics.name: device_disk_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the physical disk health of the MacroSAN storage array, derived from the per-disk health table (MACROSAN-MIB diskHealthState, one row per disk). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other state is treated as a fault, and the worst disk is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a degraded or failed disk that risks data loss and warrants immediate replacement.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed storage uplinks, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed storage uplinks, keeping the rate accurate.
